entries.action: Replace the authoritative entrypoint with an Evidence Lake release-set, selection, E6 projection, verification, and promotion driver. Keep the old wrapper only for stability reproduction until rollback expires.
retirement_gates: new top-level driver reproduces the accepted build from pinned raw and typed inputs
no accepted build manifest names a legacy cooked artifact as scientific authority
local atomic promotion and rollback drill pass
operator accepts the scientific A/B report
entries.retention: Preserve scripts in version control and preserve every referenced stability artifact.

entries.action: Remove these merged or field-losing projections from the authoritative build graph after every active source has a registered immutable raw, source-native typed, E4, and E5 route.
retirement_gates: source-release registry and field-disposition ledgers remain complete
E4 release set accounts every active source adapter or boundary disposition
clean build no longer reads legacy cooked outputs
rollback build and reports remain retained
entries.retention: Retire only reproducible cooked projections; never delete unique raw snapshots, typed evidence, manifests, citations, or reports.

entries.replacement_status: Corrected clean foundation, selected science, CORE, hierarchy, ARM, DISC, runtime bundle, public slice, map, scene, API, and browser products pass pinned-input verification and isolated reproduction without stability science. The legacy path remains only as the retained rollback reproducer until local cutover completes.
entries.action: Replace bootstrap inventory and scientific-value production with release-scoped identity, selected facts, and E6 projections. Do not retire while E6 still composes from the stability databases.
retirement_gates: E6 compiler has no stability CORE, ARM, hierarchy, or DISC database dependency
clean pinned-input reproduction preserves accepted permanent object identities and hierarchy
shared selected-fact consumers replace duplicate runtime science selection
local promotion and rollback drill pass
entries.retention: Keep the exact stability source revision and immutable input build for rollback and historical reproduction.

entries.action: Retain the release-scoped identity compiler and the content-addressed identity-only hierarchy migration seed. Adapt canonical emission to consume that seed plus E4/E5 contracts without reopening the stability databases. Preserve stable Spacegate object identities independently of every Gaia release.
retirement_gates: not_retirable_permanent_identity_work
entries.retention: Permanent identity mappings, adjudication outcomes, and hierarchy lineage are never discarded during Evidence Lake cutover.

entries.action: Shared selected facts and versioned derivations now drive clean CORE, ARM, public planet categories, simulation luminosity, API, search, and map projections. After the promotion/rollback drill, deprecate the remaining legacy scientific selectors as stability-reproduction-only; retain explicitly labeled DISC and rendering presentation transforms.
retirement_gates: all scientific consumers use shared selected facts or versioned derivations
legacy inventory audit reports no unowned algorithm marker
classification, HZ, planet category, simulation, API, search, map, tag, and AAA parity pass
entries.retention: Keep the inventory and audit permanently as supersession evidence.

entries.action: Keep the explicit refusal while old operational references may exist, then remove the option and its documentation after the rollback window.
retirement_gates: no operator documentation or automation invokes --from-cooked
accepted Evidence Lake build has completed its rollback retention window
entries.retention: The refusal can be removed; historical build reports remain.

entries.action: Keep only the bounded parity diagnostic until tiled transport has passed the Evidence Lake local promotion and rollback drill; never restore it as a second production renderer architecture.
retirement_gates: tiled public build passes production browser acceptance
local promotion and rollback preserve search, selection, Peek, Explorer, and system handoff
entries.retention: Retain benchmark reports and protocol documentation after endpoint removal.
